evals.name: install-precommit-local
evals.prompt: This is a fresh repo and I keep worrying I'll paste an API key into a file and commit it by accident. Can you set things up so that can't happen on this repo?
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes this as a prevention task and uses the install-hooks skill: it installs ggshield as a git hook for this repository (e.g. `ggshield install -m local -t pre-commit`), surfaces the local-vs-global and pre-commit-vs-pre-push choice rather than guessing silently, and verifies the install by checking the command exited 0 and the hook script exists at .git/hooks/. It does NOT treat this as a request to scan existing code (that would be scan-secrets), and does NOT claim the existing code is now clean.

assertions.text: Treats this as installing a git hook (install-hooks / `ggshield install`), not as a `ggshield secret scan` of existing code
Installs for this repository (`-m local` / `--mode local`), since the user asked about 'this repo' — does not silently install globally
Runs (or hands over) a `ggshield install` command with a hook type (`-t pre-commit` or `-t pre-push`), not some other mechanism
Presents or justifies the stage choice (pre-commit vs pre-push) rather than choosing silently with no rationale
Verifies installation by exit code and/or the presence of the hook script (e.g. `.git/hooks/pre-commit`)
Does NOT fabricate a test commit with a dummy secret or claim it proved the hook 'fires' — verification is install-level only
Does NOT claim the repo's existing code is now free of secrets; if it mentions existing code at all, it points to scan-secrets for that

evals.name: global-needs-consent
evals.prompt: I want every git repo on my machine to block secret commits automatically, not just this one. Set that up.
evals.expected_output: Agent identifies this as a global install (`ggshield install --mode global`, which writes into git's template directory and changes the user's global git configuration). Because that is system-modifying, the agent gets explicit consent before running it — explaining that it affects all future repos and touches global git config — rather than executing the global install silently. It then uses the correct global command.

assertions.text: Recognizes the request requires global mode (`-m global` / `--mode global`), not a per-repo local install
Communicates that global mode changes the user's global git configuration / affects all future repositories
Seeks explicit confirmation BEFORE running the global install, rather than executing it silently as a convenience
When it does proceed, uses a correct `ggshield install -m global -t <pre-commit|pre-push>` invocation

evals.name: already-has-hook
evals.prompt: Add ggshield secret scanning to my pre-commit hook in this repo.
evals.expected_output: The repo already has a custom pre-commit hook (a lint step). A correct agent notices that overwriting it would drop the user's existing hook, so it installs ggshield with `ggshield install -a` / `--append` (or explicitly confirms before using `-f` / `--force`). It does NOT silently overwrite the existing hook script.

assertions.text: Accounts for the pre-existing .git/hooks/pre-commit rather than assuming a clean slate
Uses `-a` / `--append` to preserve the existing hook, OR explicitly confirms with the user before `-f` / `--force` overwrites it
Does NOT silently overwrite / destroy the existing pre-commit hook
Uses `ggshield install` (with a pre-commit hook type) as the mechanism

evals.name: install-claude-code
evals.prompt: Install Claude hooks for this project so secrets get caught.
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes 'Claude' names the AI-assistant hook family, not a git hook. It installs the Claude Code hook with `ggshield install -t claude-code` (mode local for 'this project'), presents the local-vs-global choice rather than guessing global, and verifies the install by exit code and the presence of a ggshield entry in .claude/settings.json. It does NOT install a git pre-commit or pre-push hook, and does NOT treat this as a request to scan existing code (that is scan-secrets).

assertions.text: Recognizes 'Claude' as the AI-assistant hook family and installs an AI-assistant hook, NOT a git pre-commit/pre-push hook
Runs (or hands over) `ggshield install -t claude-code`, not `-t pre-commit` or `-t pre-push`
Installs for this project (`-m local` / `--mode local`) since the user said 'this project'; does not silently install globally
Presents or justifies the local-vs-global scope choice rather than choosing global silently
Does NOT install or suggest a git pre-commit/pre-push hook as the primary action for this request
Does NOT treat this as a `ggshield secret scan` of existing code

evals.name: ambiguous-asks-family
evals.prompt: Can you install hooks on this repo?
evals.expected_output: The request is bare — it names no hook family (no 'Claude'/'Cursor'/'pre-commit'/'git'). A correct agent STOPS and asks which family the user wants — git hooks (block secrets entering git history on commit/push) versus AI-assistant hooks (make an AI coding tool like Claude Code or Cursor scan its prompts and actions). It does NOT silently run a `ggshield install` with a guessed hook type.

assertions.text: Does NOT silently run `ggshield install` with a guessed `-t` type before clarifying
Asks the user which hook family they want before installing
Surfaces both options — git hooks (pre-commit/pre-push) and AI-assistant hooks (claude-code/cursor/etc.)
